name: Summer Science Program
location: 3 tracks: University of Colorado Boulder (Astrophysics), University of Indiana (Biochemistry), and other host universities

cost.notes: Need-based scholarships available; many full-need scholarships

prior_knowledge: Physics and calculus background for Astrophysics track
Biology and chemistry for Biochemistry track

essays.prompt: Why are you interested in scientific research and this specific SSP track?
tips: Show genuine scientific curiosity, not just interest in the program name

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at MIT, Caltech, and top research universities for students interested in astrophysics, biochemistry, or genomics.
outcomes.typical_results: Strong placement at selective research universities; many SSP alumni pursue STEM PhDs or research careers.

what_they_look_for: Ability to work collaboratively in teams

blurbs.short: A 6-week residential research program where students conduct original research in astrophysics, biochemistry, or genomics in small teams.
blurbs.long: SSP is one of the oldest and most respected high school science research programs. What makes SSP distinctive is its team-based approach — unlike RSI where students work individually, SSP students work in small teams of 3 on a single research project over the 6 weeks. The Astrophysics track focuses on asteroid orbit determination using observational data. The Biochemistry track focuses on enzyme kinetics. The Genomics track focuses on bioinformatics. The program culminates in a formal research symposium. SSP has a strong culture of collaboration over competition, and alumni consistently describe it as one of the most formative experiences of their high school years.

curriculum: Team-based original research, daily lab work, lectures from visiting scientists, research symposium.
